tools.name: author_list
tools.description: List all authors.

tools.name: author_resolve
tools.description: Find a author by name (fuzzy).

tools.name: document_get
tools.description: Get a document (commentary/prose_chapter/letter) by id.

tools.name: document_list
tools.description: List documents at a reference / in a book. A verse's purport = document_list(source, tokens, kind:"commentary").

tools.name: excerpt_prepare
tools.description: Generate (or fetch, if cached) the audio clip for a lecture passage and return its playable URL. This is the excerpt player's Play action — it is called for you by the player UI on demand. Agents should use excerpt_render to show the player, not call this directly. Idempotent: same track+window retu

tools.name: excerpt_render
tools.description: Let the user HEAR a lecture passage inline. Call this when the user wants to LISTEN to a specific moment in a track — e.g. from a search(types:["track"]) hit, pass its track_id plus start_ms/end_ms. Renders an inline audio player that generates the clip on demand (public share-audio, max 10 minutes)

tools.name: location_list
tools.description: List all locations.

tools.name: location_resolve
tools.description: Find a location by name (fuzzy).

tools.name: media_render
tools.description: Show/play an existing corpus VIDEO clip inline. Call this when the user wants to WATCH a clip found via search(types:["media"]) — pass the hit's media_id. Renders an inline video player.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Semantic + lexical search over the corpus. Returns verses, documents, track passages and titles matching a natural-language query, each with the id needed to fetch the full record (verse_id→verse_get, document_id→document_get, track_id+start_ms/end_ms→transcript_window; media_id→media_render to play

tools.name: source_get
tools.description: Get one book by id or code ("source_…" or "BG"/"БГ").

tools.name: source_list
tools.description: List all books (Caitanya-caritāmṛta is three sources).

tools.name: source_resolve
tools.description: Find a book by name (fuzzy): "gita", "бхагаватам", "CC Madhya".

tools.name: track_get
tools.description: Get a track (lecture/conversation): metadata, cited references, transcript/pdf availability.

tools.name: track_list
tools.description: List tracks, filterable by reference (source[+tokens] = tracks citing it), author/location/kind/date/lang. No filter ⇒ recent tracks (date desc).

tools.name: transcript_window
tools.description: Read a track's transcript around a time window (from a search track hit or track_list). Widen with pad_ms.

tools.name: verse_get
tools.description: Fetch a verse's raw fields for your own reasoning (NOT for display — to show a verse to the user, use verse_render). Returns original script + transliteration as line arrays; with lang, also the canonical translation inline plus an `alternatives` manifest of other translation kinds in that language

tools.name: verse_list
tools.description: List the verses of a book / chapter. Skips .0 chapter summaries; collapses merged verses into one item with a covers span.

tools.name: verse_render
tools.description: Show a verse to the user. THE DEFAULT way to display/read a scripture verse: renders an inline card with the original script (centered), transliteration in the requested language, the word-by-word, and the translation — all at once. Use this whenever the user asks to see, read, open, or quote a spec

tools.name: verse_synonyms
tools.description: Fetch the word-by-word ("Synonyms") as a raw {word, meaning} array for one verse translation (data — verse_render already shows the word-by-word inside the card). Defaults to the canonical rendering; pass `kind` for an alternative.

tools.name: verse_translation
tools.description: Fetch the text of ONE translation of a verse (data, not a card — to display a verse use verse_render). Defaults to the canonical (kind=canonical); pass a `kind` from verse_get's `alternatives` manifest to fetch a specific rendering.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "source_get" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: excerpt_prepare, track_get, transcript_window · Private data: document_get, document_list, search, track_list, verse_get, verse_render · Public sink: excerpt_render · Destructive: search.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: excerpt_prepare, track_get, transcript_window · Private data: document_get, document_list, search, track_list, verse_get, verse_render · Public sink: excerpt_render · Destructive: search

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
